authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / Federal Trade Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the primary federal Funeral Rule regulation and directly governs price-list disclosures, itemized statements, embalming disclosures, casket and outer burial container practices, and prohibited funeral-provider practices that pre-need service support must preserve as evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: disclosure, selected-services, statement, and prohibited-practice screening
used_for: GPL, CPL, OBCPL, itemized statement, casket, embalming, and outer burial container evidence
refusal triggers for unsupported pricing or required-purchase claims
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes Funeral Rule disclosure records hard prerequisites for pre-need case completion or customer-facing price guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC enforces the Funeral Rule and publishes official funeral-provider guidance explaining price-list, telephone price, casket, outer burial container, embalming, itemized statement, and record-retention workflows relevant to pre-need service support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pre-need service intake, disclosure validation, customer communication, and support disposition
used_for: evidence traceability for selected funeral goods and services
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from giving confident customer guidance when required disclosure or statement records are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Pre-need Q & A
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's cemetery and funeral regulator publishes direct advance-planning guidance on written pre-need plans, change boundaries, prepayment options, and license scope for funeral establishments and cemeteries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: prearrangement intake, written-plan evidence, change request screening, and prepayment-method triage
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts pre-need plan and change questions into required written records and jurisdiction-specific authority checks.

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Guide to Funeral and Cemetery Purchases
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The state regulator guide directly covers disposition instructions, price-list and itemized statement requirements, prearrangement, prepayment options, preneed trust contracts, guaranteed-price caveats, at-need charges, trust agreement expectations, and presenting pre-need agreements at time of need.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: prearrangement, prepayment option review, guaranteed and non-guaranteed item handling, at-need handoff
used_for: guaranteed price and at-need charge caveats
preneed agreement presentation and at-need service handoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports scenarios where customers ask whether old pre-need selections cover current at-need charges.

authoritative_sources.title: Our Purpose
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Banking Prepaid Funerals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Texas Department of Banking maintains this official prepaid funerals site under Texas Finance Code section 154.132. It defines the regulator's consumer-information purpose, seller permit requirements, trust-funded and insurance-funded options, and prepaid funeral merchandise and services scope.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: seller permit screening, funding-type classification, and tenant jurisdiction readiness
used_for: scope boundaries for prepaid merchandise and services
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes permit context and funding type required tenant facts before service resolution.

authoritative_sources.title: FAQs
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Banking Prepaid Funerals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Texas regulator FAQ explains prepaid funeral contract regulation, permit checks, trust-funded and insurance-funded options, modifications, cancellations, payment status, provider performance, and adjacent regulator boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: support triage, cancellation and modification intake, funding status support, adjacent regulator routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps service support from approving contract changes, refunds, or insurance outcomes while still preparing useful escalation packets.

authoritative_sources.title: Examination Manual for Insurance-Funded & Trust-Funded Prepaid Funeral Contract Permits
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The regulator's examination manual defines the recordkeeping and examination evidence for trust-funded and insurance-funded prepaid funeral contracts, including general files, reconciliations, historical register, new contract files, guaranteed and non-guaranteed items, consumer disclosures, matured files, cancellations, and policy-status records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recordkeeping, funding reconciliation, matured file review, at-need reconciliation, cancellation evidence
used_for: trust ledger, control ledger, insurance policy, and reconciliation artifacts
repeat-failure and regulator escalation output shapes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the named systems and records that distinguish this lane from generic customer support.

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Service Workers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS provides official public occupational workflow terminology for funeral service workers, including helping clients plan funerals in advance, providing service options, filing documents, and coordinating cemetery or crematory details.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: terminology, adjacent workflow context, and professional boundary setting
used_for: adjacent funeral director and cemetery coordination boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports realistic workflow language while preserving licensed and adjacent-owner handoffs.

workflow_stages.name: Tenant, jurisdiction, and authority readiness
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, establishment or cemetery role, seller permit or license context, service model, access policy, authority owner map, source systems, and tenant rules before acting on a pre-need support request.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Pre-need request and participant intake
workflow_stages.description: Capture requester, purchaser, beneficiary, authorized representative, contract number, seller, provider, funding type, intended outcome, urgency, access basis, and communication constraints.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, selection, and contract evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate written pre-need plan, executed contract, GPL/CPL/OBCPL or applicable price-list evidence, itemized selected goods and services, package, cash advance, guaranteed and non-guaranteed item classification, disclosure acknowledgements, and audit references.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Funding and status record validation
workflow_stages.description: Check trust-funded ledgers, control ledger, trustee or bank statement references, insurance policy application and data face sheet, premium or payment status, death benefit, cash surrender value, payment plan, and contract status without approving financial outcomes.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Service resolution or supported communication
workflow_stages.description: Produce a service disposition, pre-need case summary, customer communication summary, or missing-record request when documented records and tenant rules allow an operational resolution.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, change, cancellation, or complaint escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported workarounds, policy exceptions, contract modifications, cancellation benefit approvals, funding conflicts, provider-performance complaints, privacy issues, or unlicensed-sales concerns to authorized owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: At-need reconciliation and handoff
workflow_stages.description: When a pre-need contract matures, package death certificate references, assignment of benefits, provider request, at-need contract, preneed-to-at-need reconciliation, non-guaranteed item differences, potential overcharge flags, and next owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: written pre-need plan or prearrangement guide
executed prepaid funeral contract and contract number
purchaser, beneficiary, authorized representative, seller or permit holder, contracted provider, and assigned successor provider records
General Price List, Casket Price List, outer burial container price list, and itemized statement of selected goods and services
guaranteed services and merchandise, non-guaranteed cash advance items, package selections, at-need charge estimates, and consumer disclosure acknowledgements
trust-funded contract ledger, control ledger, trustee or bank statement, historical contract register, deposit or withdrawal record, and reconciliation report
insurance-funded policy application, data face sheet, policy status, premium status, death benefit, cash surrender value, irrevocable assignment, and waiver of right to cancel
cancellation notice, cancellation forms, refund computation support, withdrawal approval, evidence of payment, and policy-status change notice
death certificate reference, assignment of benefits, servicing provider request, at-need contract, preneed-to-at-need reconciliation, potential overcharge flag, and family communication log
service disposition, stakeholder communication summary, evidence packet, escalation packet, audit log, and orchestrator return note
systems_of_record: trust accounting ledger and trustee statement repository
insurance policy administration and carrier portal
payment, refund, and cancellation workflow system
audit log and compliance review system

allowed: classify pre-need service requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked
request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context
summarize contract status, funding evidence, disclosure evidence, missing prerequisites, and next owner from source records
prepare customer or stakeholder communication summaries that avoid legal, financial, insurance, and licensed authority claims
assemble pre-need evidence packets, at-need reconciliation summaries, complaint packets, and adjacent-lane handoffs
escalate: public source, tenant policy, contract terms, trust records, insurance records, or provider records materially conflict
contract modification, cancellation benefit, refund, trust withdrawal, policy status, price guarantee, or provider-performance decision requires approval
legal right of disposition, family authority, estate, Medicaid, tax, insurance advice, licensed funeral directing, cemetery authority, or regulator action determines the outcome
access, privacy, complaint, elder exploitation, deception, or unlicensed-sales facts are unresolved
refuse: request asks the lane to approve refunds, fee waivers, policy exceptions, trust withdrawals, insurance claims, contract modifications, or guaranteed price overrides
request asks for legal, tax, Medicaid, investment, estate, insurance, or benefits advice
request asks to disclose restricted pre-need, policy, payment, family, death, or complaint records without confirmed authority
request asks to treat pre-need service as generic CRM support and omit contract, funding, disclosure, purchaser, beneficiary, or at-need reconciliation evidence

domain_failure_modes: telling a family a plan is paid in full or guaranteed without executed contract, disclosure, payment, trust, or policy evidence
approving cancellation benefits, refund amounts, trust withdrawals, or insurance cash surrender values from a support desk request
changing selected goods and services after contract execution without the required cancellation, new contract, or authorized modification process
confusing purchaser, beneficiary, authorized representative, person with right of disposition, and servicing provider roles
hiding non-guaranteed cash advance items or at-need charges behind a generic pre-need status
reconciling trust ledger, control ledger, contract, and trustee statement conflicts by guessing
promising provider performance or no additional cost when payments are not current, policy status is in default, or non-guaranteed items are unresolved
releasing pre-need contracts, insurance policy data, payment records, family communications, or death documents to an unauthorized requester
using state-specific pre-need rules as universal rules without tenant jurisdiction and contract context
treating pre-need as cosmetic industry terminology instead of contract, funding, disclosure, and at-need reconciliation work

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: State-by-state pre-need contract, trust, insurance, and cancellation rules differ materially.
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Not blocking. The pack converts jurisdiction, permit, funding type, contract terms, source systems, and tenant policies into required runtime facts and refusal triggers instead of guessing a universal rule.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: Provider performance and at-need honoring obligations can depend on contract terms, funding status, death location, assigned successor, and servicing provider.
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Not blocking. The pack requires contract, provider, funding, at-need reconciliation, non-guaranteed item, and approval-owner evidence before any customer-facing resolution.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: Pre-need instructions may interact with right-of-disposition laws and family authority disputes.
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Not blocking. The pack requires legal or licensed authority handoff when disposition authority, family hierarchy, or survivor changes determine the outcome.
